summary: Verified all 5 bugfix criteria for the Dutch hero carousel. Backend pytest: 10/10 passed. Cold-cache /api/news (after clearing MongoDB image_colors AND restarting backend to drop in-memory cache) responded in 2.39s — well under 10s. Warm second call ~1s. Real items returned: RADIOGROEP (#561b00), 127 (#575757), KOODH (#001e57); no #111111 fallback colours. Colours persist in MongoDB collection image_colors (3 docs after first fetch). Frontend: document.title is exactly 'YNNCK | Yannick Gijbels'. Loading placeholder is a dark radial-gradient (not pure black). banner-section renders on first load with no manual refresh; 9 panels (3 articles x 3 loops) show RADIOGROEP/127/KOODH only — no 'Voorbeeldproject' / 'Tweede project' fallback. KOODH hover: title-wrap opacity=1, excerpt-wrap opacity=1, excerpt innerHTML contains real body HTML with 'YNNCK', 'Yannick Gijbels', 'Koodh Media Group'. No console errors on load.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py color_for(): the try/except around the MongoDB read swallows ALL exceptions silently (bare except Exception: pass) — this hides real Mongo outages. Consider logging the exception at debug level.

server.py dominant_color(): still uses synchronous requests.get with an 8s timeout inside asyncio.to_thread. Under adversarial upstream (slow image host) 3 parallel 8s waits still cap the endpoint at ~8s. Consider aiohttp or shortening timeout further for cold-load safety.

server.py: the module-level _color_cache dict grows unbounded and is not shared across worker processes. Not a bug in a single-worker deployment, but if uvicorn is scaled the persistent MongoDB layer becomes the only shared cache — fine as long as db.image_colors keeps working.

Banner.jsx: axios GET failures for /api/news fall back to FALLBACK silently. If the upstream news API is transiently empty (returns items=[]), users still see fake 'Voorbeeldproject' slides. Consider retrying or surfacing a small status message instead of switching to demo content in production.
Banner.jsx: no error/timeout indication if /api/news is slow — user sees the dark loading gradient indefinitely. Add a max-wait fallback (e.g. show FALLBACK after 15s) or a retry.
test_credentials: N/A - no auth in this app
seed_data_creation: Cleared MongoDB collection image_colors once to measure cold-cache performance. Backend regenerated 3 entries on next /api/news call (RADIOGROEP #561b00, 127 #575757, KOODH #001e57).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Bugfixes verified. Cold-load path: clear db.image_colors, `sudo supervisorctl restart backend`, then curl /api/news — should return in <5s with 3 real items and non-black colours. Frontend title lives in /app/frontend/public/index.html <title>. To hover KOODH programmatically: query [data-testid=carousel-panel], find indices where img[alt=='KOODH'] (indices 2,5,8), set scroll = idx*(clientHeight*0.58) - (clientHeight - clientHeight*0.58)/2, then page.mouse.move to the img center — after ~2s title-wrap opacity->1, excerpt-wrap opacity->1, excerpt innerHTML gets real HTML from /api/news/articles/dcb6fd81-... . Note: auto-scroll test can be tricky because after scrolling, panels can end up near the top-left where mouse at (1,1) still hovers them — always park mouse over a truly empty zone (e.g. right side of window, x>1400) when measuring idle auto-scroll.
rca of the issue: Original bug (black screen + fallback articles) was caused by synchronous per-image dominant-colour computation in /api/news with a 20s timeout, blocking the first response. Fix (verified): asyncio.gather over color_for() + 8s image timeout + MongoDB image_colors persistence upsert. Confirmed cold /api/news now 2.4s, warm ~1s. Frontend fallback branch never triggers because the API returns items>0 with real data. Browser title bug fixed in public/index.html <title>YNNCK | Yannick Gijbels</title>.
